step_by_step_thinking:
\boxed{A}
**Clinical Reasoning:** The Gleason score is directly derived from histopathologic examination and reflects the aggressiveness of prostate cancer through assessment of growth patterns; thus, histopathology determines the Gleason score. Option B is incorrect because the majority of prostate cancers occur in the peripheral zone, not the central zone. C is false since adenocarcinoma, not squamous cell carcinoma, is the most common type. D is inaccurate because prostate cancer is fundamentally hormone-dependent due to androgen receptor signaling driving tumor growth.
